expectations.text: The email is classified with multiple labels (quality, pd, plm), not just one
expectations.evidence: 'Labels: quality, pd, plm' stated explicitly, tied back to the signal table row for 'PET fail' and the failing-lab-result action-shape row.

expectations.text: Quality is treated as the parent action per the safety-first priority order, not pd
expectations.evidence: 'Primary (safety wins the parent slot): quality' — parent action opens in SJS Quality Management owned by quality-lab-coordinator; pd and plm are explicitly 'Secondary'.

expectations.text: The proposed PLM cross-flag to outlook-plm-bridge is explicitly mentioned
expectations.evidence: 'Child action — PLM cross-flag: Route the PET test result to outlook-plm-bridge to update the product's test-result fields...'

expectations.text: The proposed actions cross-reference each other
expectations.evidence: Explicit 'Cross-references' section: 'All three actions above reference each other: the parent Quality task links to the PD comment and the PLM flag; the PD comment links back to the parent; the PLM flag notes the source Quality task gid.'

expectations.text: The output is a confirmation preview, not a claim that the task/comment was already created
expectations.evidence: Header states 'NOT executed (read-only run)' and closes with 'Say "push it"... and I'll create the parent task' — clearly gated on confirmation.

claims.claim: Allure Labs is not the vendor of record for Castaway Cream PET testing — AMR Labs is
claims.evidence: Cross-checked against the run's own Asana/vendor lookups; consistent with the baseline run independently reaching the same conclusion, corroborating it.

eval_feedback.overall: Clean pass on all five assertions, and the run caught a genuine data-quality problem in the prompt itself (wrong vendor named) instead of fabricating a matching email — exactly the behavior you want before anything gets logged against a real quality record. No changes needed to this eval.
